data.title: Federal Reserve's Logan: Balance sheet can be reduced by changing regulatory rules.
data.description: Dallas Federal Reserve President Logan outlined the path and options for the Fed to shrink its balance sheet on Thursday, noting that the current system is functioning well and benefits overall financial stability. Logan stated that the Fed's current system for managing financial liquidity is designed to provide "ample" levels of reserves, and that the system is "efficient and effective." However, there are several ways to help reduce the Fed's holdings under the current system, many of which involve rules governing financial institutions' management of cash reserves. Recent internal and external research by the Fed indicates that by adjusting regulations to encourage banks to hold lower levels of reserves, the Fed could further shrink its balance sheet under the current system. Logan agreed, stating that the Fed is currently working to make reserve management "more efficient" during periods of stress. She also stated that some liquidity rules, while increasing reserves, have not improved safety because banks are reluctant to use these reserves during crises. "This is an inefficient use of the Fed's balance sheet, and we can completely avoid this," she said. (Jinshi)

data.title: CryptoQuant: Bitcoin may rebound to $71,500-$81,200 in the short term, but demand remains deeply contracted.
data.description: On-chain analytics firm CryptoQuant stated that Bitcoin demand remains in a "deep contraction" phase, with apparent demand decreasing by approximately 63,000 BTC in the 30 days leading up to the end of March, indicating that selling pressure still outweighs buying pressure. Although both ETFs and Michael Saylor's Strategy increased their buying, it was insufficient to alleviate overall pressure. ETFs saw 30-day buying of approximately 50,000 BTC, the highest since October 2025; Strategy's 30-day cumulative buying remained around 44,000 BTC. CryptoQuant pointed out that large holders ("whales" with 1,000–10,000 BTC) have turned net sellers, reducing their holdings by 188,000 BTC over the past year, confirming continued structural selling pressure. Mid-sized holders (the "dolphins" with 100–1,000 BTC) are still accumulating, but at a significantly slower pace, dropping from nearly 1 million BTC in October 2025 to 429,000 BTC. Furthermore, demand from US investors has weakened recently, with the Coinbase premium mostly negative, indicating that retail investors have not yet entered the market on a large scale. CryptoQuant suggests that if the macroeconomic environment improves, especially with a de-escalation of the US-Iran conflict, Bitcoin could rebound in the short term to the $71,500–$81,200 range, with $71,500 as initial resistance and $81,200 as the upper limit of the bear market rebound. (The Block)

data.title: Blockchain Technology Gains Traction Amid Technological Shifts
data.description:
Ethereum France leader Jerome de Tychey delivered the closing speech at EthCC[9], highlighting the transition of blockchain technology from skepticism to widespread adoption driven by efficiency and security. According to Foresight News, de Tychey emphasized that blockchain is at the intersection of technological changes such as AI and quantum-resistant encryption, positioning it as a core focus for the next 20 years.

Additionally, Jerome de Tychey announced that the next EthCC will be held from April 12 to 15, 2027, in Cannes, France.

data.title: Federal Reserve Considers Options for Reducing Balance Sheet
data.description: Federal Reserve officials are exploring various options for reducing the size of the central bank's balance sheet. According to ChainCatcher, Logan, a Federal Reserve official, stated that there are multiple interconnected strategies available for this process.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Logan Comments on Balance Sheet Expansion
data.description: The Federal Reserve's Logan has stated that expanding the Fed's balance sheet is acceptable if it meets public demand. According to ChainCatcher, this comment highlights the central bank's approach to managing its assets in response to economic needs. Logan's remarks come amid ongoing discussions about the role of the Federal Reserve in supporting the economy through monetary policy adjustments.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Logan Warns of Increased Financial Risk from Bank Reserve Cuts
data.description: Federal Reserve official Logan has expressed concerns about the potential risks to the financial system if banks are forced to reduce their reserve holdings. According to ChainCatcher, Logan highlighted that such measures could elevate the risk levels within the financial sector. The statement underscores the ongoing discussions about the balance between regulatory requirements and financial stability.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Microsoft Plans to Develop Advanced AI Model by Next Year
data.description:
Microsoft is set to develop a cutting-edge artificial intelligence model by next year, aiming to create an internal alternative to the strongest AI tools from OpenAI and Anthropic. According to Jin10, Mustafa Suleyman, CEO of Microsoft AI, emphasized the need to deliver technology at the forefront of innovation. By 2027, the goal is for the model to achieve state-of-the-art capabilities in text, image, and audio generation and response.

On Thursday, Microsoft's AI division launched a speech transcription model that reportedly outperformed competitors in benchmark tests across 11 of the 25 most commonly used languages. However, similar to previous speech and image generation models released by the division, this model is designed as an efficient professional tool, utilizing less training data compared to general models like Claude 3 Opus or OpenAI's GPT-4.

Suleyman noted that Microsoft is consolidating computing power to develop models with broader capabilities. Since October last year, the company has been expanding its computational resources using a set of Nvidia GB200 chips. He stated, "Over the next 12 to 18 months, we will gradually enhance our computing capabilities to reach cutting-edge levels."

data.title: AI model evaluation platform Yupp will cease operations; it previously raised $33 million in seed funding led by a16z.
data.description: Pankaj Gupta, CEO of the AI ​​model evaluation platform Yupp.ai, announced on the X platform that the platform will soon cease operations. The platform will remain accessible for the next 15 days, during which time users can download data. Existing users will be unable to create new conversations from this date, and new users will not be able to register. Yupp.ai launched in June 2025 and raised $33 million in seed funding last June, led by a16z. The platform allowed users to compare various AI models for free. Users could input prompts and view multiple AI-generated responses, selecting the best result to form a "preference data package." This data was used for post-training and evaluation of AI models. The platform used blockchain technology to ensure transparency in the evaluation process, rewarding users for providing feedback and providing verifiable training data to AI developers.

data.title: A U.S. federal judge suspended the Trump administration's ban on Anthropic; the U.S. Department of Justice appealed.
data.description: The Trump administration will appeal a federal judge's ruling blocking its ban on government use of Anthropic technology. The Justice Department filed a notice in court Thursday stating it will appeal a ruling last month by Judge Rita F. Lin of the U.S. District Court for the Northern District of California in San Francisco. The judge had previously suspended the government's plans to sever ties with Anthropic to maintain the status quo while the legal dispute continued, but she set aside the ruling for a week to give the government an opportunity to appeal. Anthropic filed the lawsuit challenging the Department of Defense's decision to claim its technology threatens the U.S. supply chain. In its March 26 ruling, the judge agreed with Anthropic's argument that the government's reasons for imposing the ban on national security grounds were questionable. The judge considered the move more like a "punishment" of Anthropic, as the company requested assurances that its technology would not be used to spy on Americans or for autonomous weapons deployment. (Jinshi)

data.title: Iranian Deputy Foreign Minister: We are studying charging ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz.
data.description: Iranian Deputy Foreign Minister Haider al-Gharibabadi stated that Iran is considering imposing passage fees on ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz. He also indicated that the fee standards are still under study and the specific amount has not yet been determined. (CCTV News)

data.title: Microsoft plans to launch a large-scale, cutting-edge AI model next year.
data.description: Microsoft announced plans to develop a large-scale, cutting-edge AI model next year to build its own internal solution to replace OpenAI and Anthropic's most powerful AI tools. Microsoft AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman stated in an interview, "We must deliver absolutely cutting-edge technology. By 2027, the goal is to have models that are truly state-of-the-art in text, image, and audio generation and response." Microsoft's AI division unveiled a speech transcription model on Thursday, which reportedly outperformed competitors in 11 of the 25 most commonly used languages. However, similar to the division's previous speech and image generation models, this model is a highly efficient, specialized tool, requiring less training data than general-purpose models such as Claude 3 Opus or OpenAI's GPT-4. Suleyman stated that Microsoft is consolidating computing power to build models with broader capabilities, and the company began using a set of Nvidia GB200 chips to expand its computing resources last October. He said, "Over the next 12 to 18 months, we will gradually upgrade to cutting-edge computing power." (Jinshi)

data.title: Analyst Predicts Alphabet Stock Could Hit $340 by May 2026
data.description: Analyst Vinod Dsouza has projected that Alphabet's stock might reach $340 by May 2026. According to NS3.AI, this forecast follows a recent rebound in Alphabet's shares from $271 to $294.90 over a three-day period. Dsouza's prediction is based on the 200-day Simple Moving Average (SMA), a recent Relative Strength Index (RSI) springboard effect, and the potential for the stock to move above the 50-day SMA, which is near $310.

data.title: Live prediction market Pumpcade raises $1 million in Pre-Seed funding round, led by Pump.fun.
data.description: Live prediction market platform Pumpcade announced the completion of a $1 million Pre-Seed funding round, led by Pump.fun, with participation from Foundation Capital and angel investor RadioSolace. The company plans to use the funds to expand its engineering team, execute marketing strategies, and prepare for the end of internal testing. (The Block)

data.title: International Monetary Fund: Federal Reserve has limited room for interest rate cuts in 2026
data.description: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) stated that although US inflation is expected to fall back to the Federal Reserve's 2% target in the first half of next year, policymakers have little room for interest rate cuts this year. According to the IMF's annual assessment of the US economy, IMF staff anticipate only one opportunity for a rate cut by the end of 2026. "Overall, staff believe there is limited room for policy rate cuts over the next year. More significant monetary easing would require a significant deterioration in the labor market outlook and no increase in inflationary pressures, given the recent rise in oil and commodity prices leading to upward inflation expectations," the IMF Executive Director noted in a separate statement. He added that given the Fed's current policy is close to neutral, "there is limited room for rate cuts in 2026, especially considering rising energy prices, the transmission effects of core inflation, and the upside risks to global commodity prices, which could further delay the achievement of the inflation target." (Jinshi)

data.title: Grayscale: Bullish on the long-term resilience of the crypto market; Middle East tensions and oil price shocks keep investors on the sidelines.
data.description: Grayscale, a cryptocurrency asset management firm, stated that the recent tensions in the Middle East and soaring oil prices have created a wait-and-see atmosphere in the market, with investors temporarily avoiding risky assets. Bitcoin has experienced significant volatility since the escalation of the conflict, but has generally remained within a range, demonstrating relative resilience. Grayscale believes that if the situation eases and energy prices fall, the market could quickly return to a favorable environment. In the long term, the fundamentals of the crypto market remain solid, including the continued growth of stablecoins and tokenized assets. The total supply of stablecoins has increased from approximately $20 billion in 2020 to approximately $315 billion in 2025, and the current period of uncertainty may provide long-term investors with opportunities to position themselves for the next round of growth. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Global Oil Supply Faces Potential Shortage, Citi Reports
data.description: On April 2, Citi announced that the global oil supply could face a potential shortage of 4.4 million barrels per day. According to BlockBeats, this situation could worsen to a shortage of 8 million barrels per day if some Gulf countries refuse to agree to Iran's proposed 'transit fee.'

data.title: Whale 'Loracle' Transfers and Sells HYPE Tokens
data.description: On April 2, a significant transaction involving the cryptocurrency HYPE was reported. According to BlockBeats, the whale known as 'Loracle' transferred 450,000 HYPE tokens, valued at approximately $15.5 million, from HyperEVM to HyperCore. The sale of these tokens commenced 20 minutes prior to the report.

data.title: Citi Forecasts Brent Oil Prices to Reach $95 by Q2 2026
data.description: Citi has projected that the average price of Brent crude oil will reach $95 per barrel by the second quarter of 2026 under its baseline scenario. According to Odaily, in a more optimistic scenario, the price could rise to $130 per barrel. The global oil supply is expected to face a potential shortfall of 4.4 million barrels per day. This shortage could increase to 8 million barrels per day if some Gulf countries refuse to agree to Iran's proposed 'transit fee.'

data.title: The premium of U.S. crude oil near-month futures over the next-month futures and over the seven-month futures has reached a record high.
data.description: According to market sources, the premium of near-month U.S. crude oil futures over both the next-month and seven-month futures contracts has reached record highs. (Jinshi)

data.title: AI TRENDS | U.S. Justice Department Appeals Federal Ban on Anthropic by Trump Administration
data.description: The U.S. Justice Department has filed an appeal against the federal ban imposed on Anthropic by the Trump administration. According to Jin10, the appeal challenges the restrictions placed on the AI company, which have been a point of contention since their implementation. The ban, originally justified on grounds of national security, has sparked debate over its impact on technological innovation and competition in the AI sector. The Justice Department's move signals a potential shift in policy, as it seeks to reassess the balance between security concerns and the advancement of AI technologies. The outcome of this appeal could have significant implications for Anthropic and the broader AI industry.
